abstract.t: Verifiable Convergence Protocol (VCP) defines the minimal data types and APIs required for autonomous agents and human-machine systems to achieve epistemic convergence without centralized truth arbiters. VCP is transport-agnostic, blockchain-agnostic, and model-agnostic. Any system implementing VCP is an Epistemic Execution System (EES).

section.title: Introduction
section.name: Introduction
section.t: As artificial intelligence transitions to autonomous agents capable of independent actions, the lack of a standardized mechanism for verifiable trust and epistemic convergence has become a critical bottleneck. VCP addresses this by providing a minimal protocol for claims, actions, and verifiable traces.
The key invariant is that the cost of manipulating a Claim's confidence exceeds the cost of validating it for all Claim objects: C(manipulation) > C(validation).

section.title: Terminology
section.name: Terminology
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in RFC 2119.

dl.dd: Proposed state change grounded in Claims.
JSON document returned by /.well-known/vcp for auto-discovery.

section.title: Core Endpoints
section.name: Core Endpoints
section.t: All endpoints MUST be served over HTTPS. Request and response bodies are application/json.

section.title: POST /v1/claim
section.name: POST /v1/claim
section.t: Submit a new Claim for verification.
Request: Claim object without claim_id, confidence.
Response 201: {"claim_id": "sha256:..."}
Response 400: {"error": "Invalid Claim schema"}

section.title: GET /v1/claim/{claim_id}
section.name: GET /v1/claim/{claim_id}
section.t: Retrieve a Claim and its current network confidence.
Response 200:
Response 404: {"error": "Claim not found"}

section.title: POST /v1/action/verify
section.name: POST /v1/action/verify
section.t: Verify if an Action is permitted given its basis Claims.
Request: Action object.
Response 200:
Rule: Gateway MUST reject if basis is empty or any claim_id has confidence < 0.7.

section.title: GET /v1/trace/{trace_id}
section.name: GET /v1/trace/{trace_id}
section.t: Retrieve immutable verification history for an Action.
Response 200: Trace object.
Response 404: {"error": "Trace not found"}

section.title: GET /.well-known/vcp
section.name: GET /.well-known/vcp
section.t: Returns the Discovery Document allowing clients to automatically discover capabilities, version, and endpoint locations of a VCP-compatible node.
Successful Response (200 OK):

ol.li: Agent submits Claim -> system computes confidence via GQRVP.
Agent proposes Action with basis Claims.

section.title: Security Considerations
section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: VCP guarantees that the cost of manipulating a Claim's confidence exceeds the cost of validating it.
Discovery Endpoint:

ul.li: Implementations SHOULD apply rate limiting (e.g., 10-30 requests per minute per IP).
Authentication is OPTIONAL for public nodes but RECOMMENDED for private ones.
The document MUST NOT expose sensitive data.

section.t: General:

ul.li: Quantum readiness via PQC where applicable.

section.title: IANA Considerations
section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: Media Type: application/vcp+json
Well-Known URI: vcp

section.title: Implementation Status (per RFC 7942)
section.name: Implementation Status (per RFC 7942)
section.t: The reference implementation (gravitnet) provides:

ul.li: Implemented: VCP endpoints, in-memory state, discovery via /.well-known/vcp.
Not implemented: GQRVP MWU+Gossip convergence, signature verification, cross-node state synchronization.

section.t: Testnet: A 50-node local cluster can be started using scripts/start_testnet.sh.

ul.li: Nodes run in separate containers on a single host (or GitHub Actions runner).
Each node maintains its own isolated in-memory state.
This demonstrates API-level interoperability, not a production distributed consensus network.
Full gossip-based synchronization is planned for version -01.

section.t: This testnet is a development and demonstration tool, not a production deployment.
